questions.text: The word 'Democracy' comes from which Greek word?
questions.explanation: Democracy is derived from the Greek word 'Demokratia', where 'demos' means people and 'kratia' means rule.

questions.text: In a democracy, the final decision-making power must rest with:
questions.explanation: The first feature of democracy is that final authority must lie with representatives elected by the citizens.

questions.text: In which country did Pervez Musharraf lead a military coup in 1999?
questions.explanation: General Pervez Musharraf led a military coup in Pakistan in October 1999, overthrowing a democratically elected government.

questions.text: Which of these is a feature of a democratic election?
options: One person, one vote, one value
questions.explanation: Democracy requires free and fair elections where those in power have a fair chance of losing and every vote counts equally.

questions.text: The 'PRI' (Institutional Revolutionary Party) is associated with which country?
questions.explanation: In Mexico, the PRI won every election until 2000, often using dirty tricks to defeat opposition parties.

questions.text: In China, elections are regularly held every five years for electing the country’s parliament, called:
questions.explanation: This is the National People's Congress, though only members of the Communist Party are allowed to contest.

questions.text: Until 2015, in which country did women not have the right to vote?
questions.explanation: Saudi Arabia denied voting rights to women until a decree change in 2015.

questions.text: Zimbabwe attained independence from White minority rule in:
questions.explanation: Zimbabwe became independent in 1980 and was ruled by Robert Mugabe's ZANU-PF party for decades.

questions.text: Which of the following is an argument AGAINST democracy?
options: It improves the quality of decision making
It leads to instability due to changing leaders
It provides a method to deal with differences
It allows us to correct our own mistakes
questions.explanation: A common criticism is that frequent leadership changes in democracy can lead to instability.

questions.text: Which country suffered from the worst recorded famine in world history (1958-1961)?
questions.explanation: China's famine killed nearly 3 crore people; economists argue India's democratic setup prevented a similar disaster.

questions.text: Democracy is better than other forms of government because it:
options: Allows people to enjoy complete freedom without laws
Allows us to correct its own mistakes
Ensures the rule of the smartest people
questions.explanation: Democracy provides a mechanism for public discussion and changing decisions, allowing for self-correction.

questions.text: The most common form of democracy in the modern world is:
questions.explanation: In representative democracy, people rule through their elected representatives rather than participating directly.

questions.text: A democratic government rules within limits set by constitutional law and ________.
questions.explanation: Rule of law and respect for rights are essential features of a functioning democracy.

questions.text: Who said 'Democracy is government of the people, by the people and for the people'?
questions.explanation: This famous definition was given by Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the USA.

questions.text: In Fiji, the electoral system is such that the vote of an indigenous Fiji has ________ value than that of an Indian-Fijian.
questions.explanation: This violates the principle of 'one person, one vote, one value'.

questions.text: Why can't all citizens sit together and take decisions directly in modern democracies?
options: Large population makes it physically impossible
questions.explanation: Modern democracies involve such large numbers of people that direct participation is not feasible.

questions.text: What is the Legal Framework Order issued by Musharraf in 2002?
options: An order that amended the Constitution of Pakistan
An order to go to war
An order to build new schools
questions.explanation: It gave the President the power to dismiss the national and provincial assemblies.

questions.text: Democracy enhances the ________ of citizens.
questions.explanation: Democracy is based on the principle of political equality, recognizing that even the poorest have the same status as the rich.

questions.text: One person, one vote, one value means:
options: One person can vote multiple times
Each person has one vote and each vote has one value
Only one person in a family can vote
questions.explanation: This is the fundamental principle of political equality in a democracy.

questions.text: Robert Mugabe was the leader of which party?
questions.explanation: ZANU-PF led the freedom struggle in Zimbabwe and ruled the country after independence.

questions.text: Democracy provides a method to deal with:
questions.explanation: By allowing peaceful discussion, democracy ensures no one is a permanent winner or loser.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a democratic way of taking a decision?
questions.explanation: Democratic decisions involve all those who are affected by that decision.

questions.text: The primary objective of the PRI in Mexico was to:
questions.explanation: They used unfair practices like forcing government employees to attend party meetings and shifting booths last minute.

questions.text: Estonia has made its citizenship rules in such a way that people belonging to ________ find it difficult to get the right to vote.
questions.explanation: This is an example of the denial of equal rights in citizenship.

questions.text: A democratic government is a ________ government.
questions.explanation: It is accountable to the people and must respond to their needs.

questions.text: In a democracy, political parties are allowed to:
questions.explanation: Active political competition and criticism are hallmarks of a healthy democracy.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of an un-democratic practice in Zimbabwe?
options: Changing the constitution to increase presidential powers
questions.explanation: Mugabe's government repeatedly changed the constitution to make him less accountable.

questions.text: Elections in China do not offer the people any:
questions.explanation: Since only the Communist Party can contest, there is no real alternative for the voters.

questions.text: Democracy is based on the consultation of many, which reduces the possibility of ________ decisions.
questions.explanation: Involving many people ensures that different perspectives are considered, preventing hasty errors.

questions.text: If a government is not democratic, it can still be:
questions.explanation: A non-democratic government might be efficient but it is not required by law to respond to people's needs.

questions.text: In Mexico, how often are presidential elections held?
questions.explanation: Mexico holds elections every six years to elect its President.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the 'Rule of Law'?
options: The law is whatever the leader says
Everyone is equal before the law
Only the poor follow the law
questions.explanation: Rule of law means that no one is above the law and it applies to everyone equally.

questions.text: Which of these is a requirement for a free and fair election?
options: Opposition parties should be able to contest
The ruling party should have a chance to lose
questions.explanation: These conditions ensure that the electoral process is truly democratic.

questions.text: Major decisions in Pakistan after the 2002 Legal Framework Order were taken by:
questions.explanation: Although there was an elected parliament, the real power rested with the military and Musharraf.

questions.text: What does 'Accountable' mean in a democratic context?
options: The government must answer to the citizens
questions.explanation: A democratic government is required to explain its actions and policies to the public.

questions.text: In which form of government is the ruler usually a hereditary one?
questions.explanation: In a monarchy, the right to rule is passed down through the royal family.

questions.text: Democracy is not a ________ for all socio-economic problems.
questions.explanation: While democracy is the best form of government, it does not automatically end poverty or inequality.

questions.text: Which of the following is essential for the protection of citizens' rights in a democracy?
questions.explanation: An independent judiciary ensures that the government does not violate the rights of the people.

questions.text: In a democracy, the 'Demos' refers to:
questions.explanation: As discussed, Demos is the Greek word for people.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about democracy is TRUE?
options: Democracy leads to the best decisions every time
Democracy ensures that all people are rich
Democracy is better than any other form of government
Democracy does not allow for any mistakes
questions.explanation: Compared to alternatives, democracy is the most desirable form of government because of its features and values.
